description: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) provides a policy framework of computer security guidance for how organizations can assess and improve their ability to prevent, detect, and respond to cyber attacks.
note: This scanner checks CODE-RELATED controls only. Organizational/process controls require manual assessment.

functions.name: PROTECT
functions.description: Develop and implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of critical services.

categories.name: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
categories.official_text: Access to physical and logical assets and associated facilities is limited to authorized users, processes, and devices, and is managed consistent with the assessed risk of unauthorized access to authorized activities and transactions.

code_checks.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited
Access permissions and authorizations are managed with least privilege

categories.name: Data Security
categories.official_text: Information and records (data) are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

code_checks.description: Protections against data leaks are implemented

categories.name: Information Protection Processes and Procedures
categories.official_text: Security policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage protection of information systems and assets.

code_checks.description: Configuration of systems incorporates security principles
Response and recovery plans are in place
A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented
code_checks.pass_criteria: npm audit available for dependency scanning

categories.name: Protective Technology
categories.official_text: Technical security solutions are managed to ensure the security and resilience of systems and assets, consistent with related policies, procedures, and agreements.

code_checks.description: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed
The principle of least functionality is incorporated
Communications and control networks are protected

functions.name: DETECT
functions.description: Develop and implement appropriate activities to identify the occurrence of a cybersecurity event.

categories.name: Anomalies and Events
categories.official_text: Anomalous activity is detected and the potential impact of events is understood.

code_checks.description: A baseline of network operations and expected data flows is established
Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources

categories.name: Security Continuous Monitoring
categories.official_text: The information system and assets are monitored to identify cybersecurity events and verify the effectiveness of protective measures.

code_checks.description: The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
Monitoring for unauthorized personnel, connections, devices, and software is performed
code_checks.pass_criteria: Security event logging for unauthorized access

categories.name: Detection Processes
categories.official_text: Detection processes and procedures are maintained and tested to ensure awareness of anomalous events.

excluded_functions.name: IDENTIFY
excluded_functions.reason: Organizational process - asset inventory, risk assessment, governance

excluded_functions.name: RESPOND
excluded_functions.reason: Organizational process - incident response planning, communications
